sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with vision changes and difficulty adjusting to dark environments, particularly in fancy restaurants, which he has been experiencing for approximately 3-4 months.
He reports that straight lines appear wavy to him, such as the paneling in his office and the lines on the door frame.
He also mentions that he and his wife have been eating out frequently, most recently at a restaurant called the "Italium" place, where he had an excellent dining experience.
The patient denies any fever, but mentions that he smokes about a pack a day, despite having tried various methods to quit.
He has a history of high blood pressure and has not been taking his blood pressure medication as prescribed.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of high blood pressure and smokes about a pack a day.
He has tried various methods to quit smoking, including hypnosis, lozenges, and chewing gum.
Review of systems: The patient reports difficulty adjusting to dark environments and visual disturbances, but denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: I prescribed blood pressure medication [dosage and frequency not mentioned] for hypertension.
The patient has not been taking this medication as prescribed.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The anterior segment examination revealed posterior chamber intraocular lenses in both eyes.
The cornea was clear in both eyes, with no signs of neovascularization or holes.
The fundus examination of the right eye showed the retina to be attached, with no holes or tears noted.
The posterior segment examination revealed drusen in the macula, retinal thickening, and subretinal fluid and exudate accumulation, with a hemorrhage noted.
The left eye had clear cornea, no neovascularization, and no subretinal fluid or hemorrhage.
Test Results: The diagnostic exam showed drusen deposits under the retinal pigment epithelium, subretinal fluid accumulation, and choroidal neovascular membrane.
The fluorescein angiography revealed a focal area of leakage in the right eye consistent with neovascular age-related macular degeneration.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has neovascular age-related macular degeneration (AMD), also known as wet macular degeneration.
Problem list: Neovascular age-related macular degeneration; hypertension; smoking.
Plan: I will administer an injection of Lucentis in the vitreous portion of the eye once a month to slow the rate of vision decline and potentially stop it.
I also want the patient to take his blood pressure medication as prescribed.
Follow-up: The patient will be scheduled for a follow-up appointment to receive the first injection, and subsequent injections will be scheduled on a monthly basis.
